title: Glossary of Time
description: An incomplete glossary of time. Exploring the gaping holes in the lexicon of time that we don’t even realise we are missing.

bodyText.text: Dear reader,More than what we can do with words, this glossary uncovers what words can do with us. These words give form

sample.text:
Incomplete Glossary of Time
            
            
                
                    About
                    Sug
Dear reader,More than what we can do with words, this glossary uncovers what words can do with us. These words give form
the bittersweet nostalgia for a time you never actually lived, fueled by aesthetics, literature, or music.
the exhilarating feeling of time seeming to speed up as you get older.
the act of mindlessly scrolling through social media, often resulting in the feeling that time has been wasted.
the feeling of time inexplicably slipping away, often during moments of boredom or repetitive tasks.
a longing for the structure and predictability of a past schedule, even if it wasn't necessarily enjoyable.
the dizzying feeling of being overwhelmed by the vastness of time yet to come.
a fondness for the melancholy or beauty inherent in the impermanence of time.
the peculiar dilation of time in the moment just before a sneeze, where the sensation of inevitability makes the world b
to experience a temporal misjudgment, mistaking the hour as later than it truly is, often caused by the early descent of
the sudden realization that your uplifting happiness is sparked by someone you don’t know personally, yet whose presence
the timeless, almost trance-like state of losing track of time while immersed in the sensory world of a shower.
a person raised in tropical regions, whose sense of time remains tethered to the steady equatorial rhythm of sunlight, l
the deliberate and heightened awareness of stretching time through focused concentration, where each moment feels elonga
a bittersweet feeling of festivity inspired by celebrations happening far away, often tied to a nostalgic longing for th
the disorienting state of being caught between lapses of sleep and wakefulness, where time feels suspended and unreal.
relating to the melancholic transition from sunset to dusk, when the light fades and the mood grows introspective.
the pervasive feeling that life is caught in an endless cycle, where each moment echoes a past one and the future feels
to experience the disorienting compression of time during winter evenings, when darkness arrives prematurely and skews t
the fleeting perception of time as mere intervals between grand meals, characterized by a sense of lingering fullness an
the heightened temporal awareness tied to fertility and reproductive choices, marked by an almost tangible internal tick
sensitivity to time shaped by seasonal variations in light and shadow, particularly in intimate or familiar environments
the unquantifiable, fluid space between measurable moments, where human consciousness, reflection, and spirit truly resi
a deep and visceral awareness of humanity's precarious future that consumes the ability to dream beyond the immediate pr
a state of fragmented and incomplete perception of the future, where time feels like a tangled, unfinished tapestry—disj
the emotionally charged and hazy moments immediately preceding a significant event, filled with anticipation, possibilit
the exhilaration of gaining hours as you travel westward, feeling as though time stretches joyfully in your favor.
the melancholy of losing hours as you travel eastward, as if time contracts and leaves you with a sense of loss.
the surreal, dreamlike state of disorientation when dusk is mistaken for dawn after slipping from a nap into deep slumbe
the absorption of the atmosphere and prevailing sense of time from one's surroundings, leading to a temporary alignment
the joyful anticipation for a specific moment in the future, so intense it feels almost like a separate experience.
a choric rupture in shared time, initiated by a yawn; the contagious wave that briefly hijacks the internal rhythms of a
This glossary offers ways of 'feeling' time and, more importantly, naming what we feel, which is fundamental to sense-ma
